description: Lock high-temperature superconductors to the established 1986 ceramic/cuprate materials breakthrough rather than a 2035 future forecast.

checks.reason: Bednorz and Muller reported high-temperature superconductivity in the Ba-La-Cu-O system in 1986.
Earlier low-temperature superconductivity is the research lineage, not a physical component prerequisite.
Solid-state materials research enabled the oxide/perovskite breakthrough.
The original work required prepared mixed oxide ceramic compositions.
Quantum theory is the shared explanatory foundation for superconductivity, not a direct hard prerequisite edge.
High-temperature superconductors were discovered in 1986 without nanotechnology being a direct prerequisite.
